title: Manager-to-employee messaging
body:
## Summary
Add the ability for managers to send messages directly to individual employees within the app.

## Motivation
Managers need a way to communicate with employees about schedule changes, shift reminders, and other work-related topics without relying on personal phone numbers or external apps.

## Requirements
- Manager can send a message to any employee from the dashboard
- Employees receive messages in an inbox or notification panel
- Message history is preserved for both parties

title: Employee group chat
body:
## Summary
Add a built-in group chat so employees can communicate with each other.

## Motivation
Employees often need to coordinate informally — asking for rides home, requesting shift trades, or general team communication. A built-in chat removes the need for a separate group text or app.

## Requirements
- Shared team chat visible to all employees at the restaurant
- Optional: direct messages between individual employees
- Messages should be lightweight and real-time

## Notes
Nice-to-have feature. Lower priority than manager messaging.

title: Student profile: class schedule and graduation date
body:
## Summary
Prompt new users to indicate whether they are a student during account creation. If yes, collect their expected graduation date, class schedule, and finals schedule.

## Motivation
Many employees are students. Knowing their class and finals schedules helps managers avoid scheduling conflicts and plan ahead for graduation turnover.

## Requirements
- During registration, ask if the user is a student
- If yes, collect: expected graduation date, weekly class times, and finals dates
- Store this info on the employee profile so it can be updated anytime
- Send a quarterly reminder (e.g. start of each quarter) prompting students to update their class schedule

title: Calendar-style schedule view
body:
## Summary
Rework the employee and manager schedule views to look more like a traditional calendar rather than the current grid layout.

## Motivation
A calendar layout is more intuitive and familiar — easier to scan at a glance and understand the week/month ahead.

## Requirements
- Monthly and weekly calendar views
- Shifts displayed as blocks on the calendar
- Click into a day to see shift details

title: Auto-discover local events and weather for scheduling
body:
## Summary
Replace the manual event entry with an automated system that discovers upcoming events in Davis and displays local weather — helping managers anticipate busy periods they might not already know about.

## Motivation
The current event feature only lets managers log events they already know about. The real value is surfacing things they *don't* know — a downtown festival, a baseball game, graduation weekend — so they can staff accordingly.

## Requirements
- Web scraper or API integration that pulls upcoming events in Davis (e.g. downtown events, UC Davis sports, community calendars)
- Weather forecast integration (daily high/low, conditions)
- Display events and weather on a calendar view on the manager dashboard
- Manager can flag an event as high/medium/low impact for staffing

title: Custom shift times and closing shifts
body:
## Summary
Replace the fixed morning/afternoon/night shift slots with fully custom start and end times. Add support for a "closing" shift type where the end time varies by night.

## Motivation
Restaurant shifts don't fit neatly into three buckets. Managers need to define exact hours (e.g. 10:30 AM – 3:00 PM), and closing time changes depending on the night and when the last customers leave.

## Requirements
- Manager can set arbitrary start and end times when creating a shift
- Add a "closing" shift option where the end time is flexible or set after the fact
- Shift display should reflect the actual times, not fixed labels

title: Payroll tracking: hours worked and hourly rates
body:
## Summary
Add basic payroll visibility so managers can see how many hours each employee has worked and what they earn per hour.

## Motivation
Managers need to track labor costs and ensure employees are getting the right number of hours. Currently there is no way to see this information in the app.

## Requirements
- Store hourly pay rate on each employee's profile
- Calculate total hours worked per week/pay period from shift assignments
- Manager dashboard view showing hours and estimated pay per employee
- Optional: flag overtime (hours exceeding a threshold)
